outcome_contract.why: Process-improvements pages are the operator's daily work surface. Currently passive report feel — stale counts, internal jargon, flat lists — undermines daily use. Pages need accurate live state and a command-centre look to be genuinely useful.
outcome_contract.intended_outcome_statement: Redesigned `/process-improvements/*` pages with: (a) zero data-accuracy regressions on the 5 identified bugs, (b) scoped dark command-centre visual theme, (c) spatial dashboard layout with urgency-tiered sections, and (d) all internal-system copy replaced with operator-facing plain language.

engineering_coverage.applicable: Phase-by-phase: P1 minimal change; P2 new CSS utilities + class scoping; P3 full component restructure
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: All changes land together — harder to isolate visual regressions
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Phase 2+3 own all visual work; DS token compliance verified per phase. New `@utility` blocks confined to `global.css`

engineering_coverage.applicable: P1 fixes data bugs without touching markup; P2 re-skins all states; P3 adds new section states (collapsed deferred, swimlane empty states)
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Risk that dark-theme states are incomplete in a combined pass
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Each state (loading, empty, error, pending, bulk-select, snooze-picker) explicitly re-skinned in P2; new swimlane empty states added in P3

engineering_coverage.applicable: N/A in all options — internal operator tool, no PII, no auth changes
N/A — no changes to decision ledger or API
P1: regression assertions for B1–B5; P2: snapshot updates; P3: snapshot updates
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Single combined snapshot update masks which changes caused failures
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Per-phase test updates — P1 adds regression assertions, P2+P3 update snapshots. Existing TC-07/08/09 must pass throughout

engineering_coverage.applicable: P1: `initialInProgressCount` prop removed from `NewIdeasInboxProps`; `ProcessImprovementsSummary` prop signature updated; `InboxSection` gains explicit `count` prop
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Contract changes mixed with visual changes — harder to catch type regressions
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: P1 owns all interface changes. `pnpm typecheck` must pass after P1 before P2 starts

engineering_coverage.applicable: Scoped CSS vars: zero-cost. Sub-nav adds one `useEffect` + `fetch` on mount (30s poll). No new heavy dependencies
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: The sub-nav poll is the only new network request; it reuses the same endpoint already polled by both inbox components

engineering_coverage.applicable: Three independently revertable commits. Each phase is CI-green before the next starts.
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Single revert but a much larger blast radius
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: P1 → P2 → P3 commit sequence. Production Workers deployment — each commit must pass full CI before dev deployment

stage_payload.decision_summary: The `/process-improvements/new-ideas` and `/process-improvements/in-progress` pages need two things: accurate data (5 confirmed bugs) and a visual identity shift from flat admin report to living command centre. The analysis resolves three strategic choices: (1) how to apply the dark theme without breaking the rest of BOS; (2) how to fix stale counts without touching the lib layer; (3) whether to do a full spatial layout restructure or just re-skin the existing flat list. All three choices interact — the sequencing recommendation is that the three choices form three coherent build phases with c
goals: Fix 5 confirmed data-accuracy bugs (B1–B5): stale counts, hardcoded zero, React children count, SSR-locked badge, internal copy labels.
Resolve visual artefact V1 (hero-foreground tokens outside hero context) as a natural consequence of dark theme application.
Apply scoped dark command-centre theme — navy/indigo dark palette, gradient hero area, glow utilities — without touching rest of BOS.
Restructure page layout: hero summary strip with live stats; urgency-tiered swimlanes (overdue → operator actions → queue → deferred collapsed → done timeline); sub-nav with live counts and pulse.
All existing interactive behaviour (do/defer/decline/mark-done/snooze/bulk) preserved exactly.

evaluation_criteria.Why it matters: Wrong numbers break operator trust immediately
Primary goal — command-centre feel, not incremental improvement
All triage actions must continue to work; regression here is blocking
Three-phase scope; each phase must be CI-green independently
Mandatory constraint — no hardcoded values, DS atoms used for controls
Production BOS deployment; each commit must be individually revertable

options_considered.Option: **A: Three-phase sequential** — data fixes → theme/CSS → layout restructure
options_considered.Description: Each phase has a clean, independently reviewable diff. Data bugs fixed first establishes accuracy baseline before visual work.
options_considered.Upside: Lowest blast radius per commit; easiest CI debugging; each phase can ship independently
options_considered.Downside: Slightly more elapsed time than a single pass
options_considered.Key risks: Theme phase touches CSS and markup simultaneously — still complex but contained

options_considered.Option: **B: Single combined pass** — data + theme + layout in one PR
options_considered.Upside: Everything working together is visually cleanest
options_considered.Downside: Any CI failure blocks the entire redesign; mixed diffs are hard to review; regressions harder to bisect
Does not deliver the "command centre" feel — flat list with dark colours is still a boring dark list. Misses the goal.
Light-surface spatial layout is just a reorganised admin report. The visual energy comes from the dark palette. The two are complementary; splitting them weakens both.

options_considered.Description: CSS custom property overrides scoped to a wrapper class in `global.css`. DS atoms pick up overrides automatically via `hsl(var(--color-*))`. Possible brief flash of light background on initial load before CSS resolves.
options_considered.Upside: **Recommended** — matches existing `bg-hero-contrast` utility pattern; zero runtime cost; reversible by removing one class

options_considered.Description: Prevents flash entirely; requires layout-level server component change and a dedicated CSS selector `[data-theme="cmd"]`. More complex — affects the full HTML document scope.
options_considered.Upside: Over-engineered for one internal page section; flash risk is minimal in practice for an internal operator tool

options_considered.Description: Highest specificity; no class scope needed

options_considered.Option: **D1: Remove `initialInProgressCount` prop; derive all counts client-side**
options_considered.Description: All count badges (B1, B2, B3, B4) become client-derived from the data already in-flight (auto-refresh returns `activePlans` and `items`). No API changes. No SSR prop threading.
options_considered.Upside: **Recommended** — simplest, self-consistent, no new network requests

options_considered.Option: **D2: Extend API to return pre-computed counts**
options_considered.Description: API computes and returns counts server-side; clients use them
options_considered.Upside: Adds server surface; counts still stale until next poll; D1 is strictly simpler
More complex; brings in SWR or manual focus listener; no advantage over D1

chosen_approach.The `/api/process-improvements/items` en: 22–27`) — sub-nav, `InProgressInbox` B4 fix, and the new-ideas hero stats all depend on this payload shape. `InProgressInbox` must expand its type assertion from `{ activePlans }` to the full payload to enable the B4 fix.

rejected_approaches: **Option B (single pass)** — combines all changes into one diff; CI failures block the entire redesign; regressions impossible to bisect. Rejected for reviewability and rollback safety.
**Option C (theme-only)** — dark flat list is still a boring list. Misses the command-centre goal. Rejected.
**Option D (layout without theme)** — spatial layout on a light surface is still a report. The visual energy comes from the dark palette. The two are architecturally coupled. Rejected.
**Sub-option A2 (HTML-level `data-theme`)** — over-engineered for the use case; flash risk is negligible for an internal operator tool on a fast local network. Rejected.
**Sub-option D2 (API count extension)** — adds server surface with no advantage over client derivation when the data is already in the refresh response. Rejected.
